description:
GOAL: Bounded loop unrolling for self-recursive fns.

Status when authored: speculative. Mentioned in fz-ul4.11.15 ("Recursion:
don't inline self-recursive directly; can do bounded unrolling separately").

## What this looks like

  For a fn `loop(n, acc) -> if n == 0 then acc else loop(n-1, step(acc))`,
  unroll the tail-recursive call up to K times (e.g., K=4):

  Pseudo-IR after unroll:
    loop(n, acc):
      if n == 0 then return acc
      a1 = step(acc)
      if n == 1 then return a1
      a2 = step(a1)
      if n == 2 then return a2
      ...
      tail_call loop(n - K, aK)

## Benefits

  - Amortizes loop-header overhead.
  - Composes with .11.15 inlining: each unrolled `step(...)` call is a
    fresh inlinable site, so step's body gets fused K times.

## Trade-offs

  - Code bloat. Need a budget.
  - Currently no profile data, so K is static.

## Sketch

  - Detect self-recursive tail-call structure: a block whose terminator is
    `TailCall(self, ...)` reached from an If branch.
  - Clone the loop body K-1 times, threading the new vars.
  - Final iteration's TailCall becomes the recurrence point.

## Dependencies

  - Logically follows fz-ul4.11.15 (inliner uses unrolled body). Not strictly
    blocked, but ordering is natural.

## Acceptance

  - One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.27.
title: Bounded recursion unrolling
